Title: Reprogramming yeast metabolism for customized starch-rich micro-grain through low-carbon microbial manufacturing

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of GDB1 increased starch accumulation by reducing glycogen/starch degradation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; starch quantification under acetate cultivation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GDB1 encodes glycogen debranching enzyme. 2. Deletion in starch-producing backgrounds increased starch content. 3. Its removal contributed to the high-starch ST1266 engineering strategy.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: acetate as sole carbon source; nutrient-deficient stationary phase relevant to starch catabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in catabolism rather than biosynthesis; included as a core experimental target in Results.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics PRJNA1226898; metabolomics MTBLS12252 (strain-level omics, not gene-specific)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful deletion target to reduce stored glucan mobilization and improve microbial starch accumulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of TPS1 increased starch accumulation, likely by reducing trehalose synthesis and conserving UDP-glucose for starch formation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS1 encodes trehalose-6-phosphate synthase. 2. Deletion increased starch content in engineered strains. 3. It was part of the combinatorial strategy producing ST1266.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents competitive carbon diversion from UDP-glucose.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Deletion can redirect carbon from trehalose toward starch in microbial engineering.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of TPS3 increased starch accumulation, likely by reducing trehalose synthesis and preserving UDP-glucose for starch biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS3 encodes trehalose-6-phosphate synthase/phosphatase-associated trehalose pathway function as described by authors. 2. Deletion elevated starch in engineered strains. 3. It was used with GDB1/TPS1 manipulations to enhance starch production.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported together with TPS1 as trehalose 6-phosphate synthase/phosphatase-encoding genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Deletion can reduce carbon competition with starch synthesis.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Moderate downregulation of ACC1 improved starch accumulation by decreasing lipid synthesis and reallocating cellular resources toward starch.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter swapping; CRISPRi; starch quantification; transcriptomics/metabolomics correlation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACC1 was downregulated in high-starch strains at the omics level. 2. Promoter swapping to a specifically low expression level improved starch in low-starch backgrounds. 3. Fine-tuning rather than strong repression was required.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CRISPRi of ACC1 in high-starch strains did not further improve starch; dosage-sensitive effect.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: weak promoter swapping for downregulation; constitutive promoter context not specified
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A tunable target for redirecting acetyl-CoA away from lipids toward starch, but requires precise expression control.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive downregulation likely impairs growth and engineering in high-starch strains was difficult.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced FAS activity via cerulenin treatment increased starch accumulation in a strain background-dependent manner, supporting lipid-to-starch resource reallocation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: chemical inhibition with cerulenin; starch quantification; omics correlation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAS expression was downregulated in high-starch strains. 2. Chemical inhibition by cerulenin increased starch in certain strain backgrounds. 3. Lipid synthesis competes with starch accumulation for cellular resources.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: acetate as sole carbon source; cerulenin treatment 5 or 10 µM
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter-swapping downregulation was unsuccessful in high-starch strains; effect is context dependent.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate resource-allocation lever to improve starch, but careful tuning is necessary to avoid growth defects.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth inhibition by cerulenin; strong repression may be deleterious.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ST501/ST587 and related engineered strains derived from W29
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies activated glucosyl donor precursor for starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose-1-phosphate and ATP into ADP-glucose, the activated donor for starch polymerization.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control step by necessity of high-level SBP expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of E. coli glgC as part of heterologous SBP markedly increased starch accumulation in acetate-grown Y. lipolytica.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway integration; copy-number optimization; starch quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E. coli SBP genes increased starch accumulation over wild type. 2. Combining one E. coli SBP copy with three C. sphaeroides SBP copies generated the best producer ST587. 3. High-level SBP expression was essential for efficient acetate-to-starch conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: acetate as sole carbon source; commercial or electrosynthetic acetate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Introduced together with starch synthase as SBP; individual contribution not separated experimentally.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GPD; TEF (strong constitutive promoters under acetate condition)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq promoter-screening PRJNA1226773; transcriptomics PRJNA1226898; metabolomics MTBLS12252
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core module for microbial starch engineering from acetate and potentially other low-carbon feedstocks.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ST501/ST587 and related engineered strains derived from W29
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms starch polymer from activated glucosyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to an α-1,4-glucan chain, elongating starch polymer.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as part of key control SBP module
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous expression of starch synthase within SBP substantially increased starch production in acetate-grown yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway integration; copy-number optimization; starch quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Heterologous starch synthase was required in the introduced SBP. 2. E. coli-derived SBP supported elevated starch accumulation. 3. Combined-origin SBP optimization maximized starch levels.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Introduced as a pathway pair with glgC; gene symbol not specified in text.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq promoter-screening PRJNA1226773; transcriptomics PRJNA1226898; metabolomics MTBLS12252
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential component of the synthetic starch pathway for building starch-rich microbial cell factories.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ST503/ST587 and related engineered strains derived from W29
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies activated glucosyl donor precursor for starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose-1-phosphate and ATP into ADP-glucose for starch biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control step by strong phenotype and copy-number optimization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SBP from C. sphaeroides increased starch production; three copies combined with one E. coli SBP copy yielded the best-performing ST587 strain.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway integration; copy-number optimization; starch quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C. sphaeroides SBP substantially improved starch accumulation. 2. Multi-copy implementation strengthened production. 3. Optimal mixed-origin SBP design was decisive for high starch titer/content.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: acetate as sole carbon source; commercial or electrosynthetic acetate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies SBP origin and dosage as major determinants of starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq promoter-screening PRJNA1226773; transcriptomics PRJNA1226898; metabolomics MTBLS12252
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme source for constructing superior acetate-to-starch pathways in yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ST503/ST587 and related engineered strains derived from W29
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose onto a growing α-glucan chain to produce starch.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as part of key SBP module
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: C. sphaeroides-derived starch synthase within SBP strongly enhanced starch accumulation in Y. lipolytica, especially in the optimized multi-copy configuration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway integration; copy-number optimization; starch quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C. sphaeroides SBP was functional in yeast. 2. Increased copy number contributed to top-performing strain ST587. 3. This module directly enabled high-level starch polymer formation from acetate-derived precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene symbol not given in text.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq promoter-screening PRJNA1226773; transcriptomics PRJNA1226898; metabolomics MTBLS12252
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Effective enzyme source for high-level microbial starch polymerization.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: storage capacity/cell morphology affecting starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MHY1 positively influenced starch accumulation by promoting hyphal morphology and enlarged cell size; deletion limited size and reduced starch, whereas overexpression stimulated hyphae and increased starch.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent morphology regulator that alters cellular storage space for starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: stress-responsive developmental cues (as described for gene annotation)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: High-starch strains showed upregulation of hyphal development-related TFs, supporting morphology engineering logic.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deleting MHY1 abolished hyphae and reduced cell size and starch content. 2. Overexpressing MHY1 stimulated hyphal growth and improved starch accumulation. 3. Cell morphology is a causal engineering lever for starch-rich micro-grain formation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for tuning cell morphology and storage capacity in microbial starch biomanufacturing.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with hyphal development; overexpression promotes hyphae
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion limits size and starch; excessive hyphal growth may alter process properties though not explicitly quantified.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression in engineered strain ST1278 increased hyphal growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; overexpression; microscopy; starch quantification

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of SRG1 stimulated hyphal growth, enlarged cell size, and increased starch accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: morphology-associated regulator with measurable positive effect on starch when deleted
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: deletion relieves repression or otherwise promotes hyphal development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified through targeted morphology engineering rather than omics-led prioritization.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SRG1 deletion promoted hyphal growth. 2. Cell enlargement correlated with increased starch content. 3. Morphological state influences intracellular starch storage capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Deletion target for enhancing storage capacity via morphology engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major food ingredient and energy source for human nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of MBP1 stimulated hyphal growth, enlarged cells, and increased starch accumulation, contributing to the top-performing strain ST1271.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key morphology regulator used in the best starch-producing engineered strain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: deletion alters morphogenesis toward hyphal growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation via SBF-associated DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Swi4p; Swi6p (as SBF complex annotation in figure legend)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ST1271 combined MBP1 deletion with SBP implementation and metabolic rewiring, reaching highest shake-flask starch content.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MBP1 deletion promoted hyphal morphology. 2. The morphology change increased starch accumulation. 3. This manipulation was part of strain ST1271, the highest starch accumulator in shake flask cultures.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to improve starch-rich micro-grain production through morphology control.
